methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Signaloid is a seed-stage semiconductor company that has raised $10M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparative set of 11 companies: 3 public semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip firms including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

Two methods yield applicable results. Stage-progression applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $10M raised proxy, implying a $25M valuation reflective of typical seed-to-next-stage expansion. Peer-median triangulation uses four same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables: Etched ($5.0B Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), and Rain AI ($500M Series A). The median across this set is $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 22.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied; Signaloid's revenue is not disclosed, limiting this approach.

The two applicable methods produce a wide triangulated range: $21M low-end (stage-progression floor) to $1.7B high-end (peer-median anchored upward). The median across both methods is approximately $762M. The substantial disparity reflects the early stage and lack of revenue visibility. Key uncertainties include product maturity, addressable market traction, and whether Signaloid's technology trajectory justifies peer valuations skewed toward later-stage, higher-visibility firms. The range supports valuations between $250M–$1.2B as most defensible absent material new data.
